item.title: Perianal Crohn-like disease in infancy revealing multisystem Langerhans cell histiocytosis.
Sarcopenia in pediatric gastroenterology and hepatology: an updated review.
item.description: Clinical and experimental pediatrics - Sarcopenia-the pathologic loss of skeletal muscle mass and strength-is increasingly recognized in pediatric gastroenterology and hepatology as an important determinant of clinical outcomes. Althoug...

item.title: Hyperamylasaemia in paediatric inflammatory bowel disease: Aetiology, outcomes and genetic determinants.
item.description: Journal of pediatric gastroenterology and nutrition - A subset of patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) develop hyperamylasaemia. This study examines its causes, clinical outcomes and the impact of amylase gene copy number variation (CNV) usi...

item.title: Autoimmune liver diseases and overlap syndromes in children with inflammatory bowel diseases.
item.description: European journal of pediatrics - Review - Autoimmune liver diseases (AILDs)-including autoimmune hepatitis (AIH), primary sclerosing cholangitis (PSC), and autoimmune sclerosing cholangitis (ASC)/AIH-PSC overlap-are clinically relevant ext...

item.title: Upper Gastrointestinal Biopsy Findings in Pediatric Inflammatory Bowel Disease: Histologic and Clinicopathologic Correlations.
item.description: Pediatric and developmental pathology : the official journal of the Society for Pediatric Pathology and the Paediatric Pathology Society - We aimed to evaluate histologic findings in upper gastrointestinal (UGI) biopsies of pediatric inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) patients, their correlation with endoscopic features, and association...
